description: Gives a screen's closing action the full column width, so a button inside a centred reveal still matches the bars on Home instead of shrinking to its label.
name: ActionBar

description: The app's one button: a full-width bar in the display face, filled sage for the thing you came to do and outlined for the quieter alternative. Dims when disabled — the state the ask button holds until a question is written.
name: ActionButton

description: The question kept on screen above the card that answers it, so the meaning is read against what was actually asked rather than from memory.
name: AskedQuestion

description: Home's hero — three cards fanned face-up. Fixed faces, not a random draw, so Home looks identical every launch; a shuffling hero reads as a bug rather than as magic.
name: CardFan

description: The spread of 22 face-down cards you move through and choose from, by arrow or swipe, wrapping at both ends. Movement is animated because every card shows the same back — without the motion the arrows read as broken since only the counter changes.
name: CardPicker

description: What a card says once face-up: quote then meaning, each set off by a row of three stars. Carries no arcana or name line — the artwork already has all three, and repeating them pushed the quote below the fold.
name: CardReveal

description: The card at the size the app shows it, with the tap-me nudge underneath. The nudge appears only while the card is face-down AND tappable — on a self-turning card it would be an instruction you cannot follow.
name: CardStage

description: The lede and the spread together — the choose-your-card step both draw flows share.
name: ChooseCardStep

description: How the daily card ends: a note that tomorrow brings another, and — only once there is a trail worth looking at — the way into it.
name: DailyOutcome

description: Carta del día — one card per day, chosen from the date itself so it cannot be rerolled. Arrives face-down on the first visit of the day and stays open on every visit after, with a way into the past cards once there are any.
name: DailyScreen

description: One arcana in the gallery grid: its artwork, face-up and tappable. No visible label — the grid is read by the images and the name waits on the reading you tap through to; the accessibility label carries arcana and character.
name: GalleryCard

description: The whole deck as a wrapped flex-row grid — 22 arcana in fixed order. Derives cell width from the available width and a fixed column count so the gutters hold on any screen size; a plain wrap rather than a FlatList so it does not fight the screen's ScrollView.
name: GalleryGrid

description: Todas las cartas — the whole deck browsable. Composes the screen header, title and intro over a GalleryGrid of all 22 arcana face-up; tapping a card deep-links to its /reading. Read-only: shows the fixed deck, draws and stores nothing.
name: GalleryScreen

description: What the history shows before there is any: not an error, just a day that has not turned over yet.
name: HistoryEmpty

description: One past day in the trail, opening in place rather than pushing a screen — the list is the point, and the meanings run long enough that navigating away for each would make the trail tedious.
name: HistoryRow

description: Cartas anteriores — the trail of daily cards already lived through, newest first, each row opening in place rather than pushing a screen.
name: HistoryScreen

description: The three ways in, in the web app's order: the two draws lead as filled bars and the daily card follows as the outlined one, since it is the habit rather than the ask.
name: HomeActions

description: Home's top line: the language switch, the greeting, a star. Equal-width flanks keep the greeting optically centred in either language.
name: HomeHeader

description: El Carot's landing screen: greeting, wordmark, a fan of cards, and the three ways in — an open message, a specific question, and the daily card, in the web app's order.
name: HomeScreen

description: Holds the ES/EN preference in context and restores it from storage on launch, rendering in the default language first so the preview never blanks waiting on a stored value. Living in context is what lets the toggle on one screen update every screen at once.
name: LangProvider

description: The ES / EN switch for card text, with the inactive half dimmed. Home only, so a reading is read rather than reconfigured.
name: LangToggle

description: The quiet centred paragraph under a title — the instruction read once before doing the thing, deliberately not competing with the cards below it.
name: Lede

description: The dim behind the slide-in menu — a full-screen Pressable that closes the menu on tap, its black overlay fading in on the opacity NavMenu passes from the shared open animation.
name: MenuBackdrop

description: The top of the menu panel: a small uppercase label and a × to close. The × does the same thing as tapping the backdrop, kept as an explicit affordance on a panel this wide.
name: MenuHeader

description: One row in the menu — a route you can tap to, with a ruled line above so the items read as a stacked list. The label arrives already localised; the row draws it and reports the tap.
name: MenuItem

description: Holds the app menu open/closed state in context so the header star and the root-mounted overlay, which live in different parts of the tree, share one source of truth rather than per-screen state.
name: MenuProvider

description: The app menu: a panel that slides in from the right over any screen, opened by the header ✳. Mounted once at the app root and driven by shared menu context; one Animated value fades the backdrop and slides the panel, kept mounted through the close so the slide-out is seen. Composes MenuBackdrop, MenuHeader and a MenuItem per route.
name: NavMenu

description: Where the question gets written. Nothing is drawn until it is: the submit button stays disabled on an empty field rather than quietly drawing for a question never posed.
name: QuestionForm

description: Tengo una pregunta específica — write the question, then choose a card from the spread with the question kept on screen above it. The question never leaves the device; it only frames the card.
name: QuestionScreen

description: Quiero recibir un mensaje — an open draw with nothing asked of it. The spread opens face-down and you choose a card yourself; it is never handed to you. A `?n=` deep link forces a specific card and skips the spread.
name: ReadingScreen

description: App shell: registers the bundled fonts, mounts the language provider and safe-area frame, and hosts the expo-router Stack. Renders no UI of its own.
name: RootLayout

untestabilityReason.justification: Framework boundary — font registration, provider mounting and the expo-router Stack. It has no visual surface of its own; what it wires up is exercised through every screen scenario.

description: The page frame every screen sits in: dark ground, scrollable, centred column. Optionally centres content in the viewport — Home wants that, the reading screens do not, because their content outgrows the viewport once a card opens.
name: Screen

description: The bar every screen below Home wears: back, the EL CAROT wordmark, a star. Carries no language toggle by design — that lives on Home.
name: ScreenHeader

description: The small line directly under a screen title — the daily card's date, and anything else qualifying the title rather than restating it.
name: ScreenSubtitle

description: A screen's own title in the display face, the line under the wordmark saying which part of the app you are in. Wraps to two lines on the longer message-flow question.
name: ScreenTitle

description: The ✳ in the header — the button that opens the app menu. Shared by both headers so they wire one handler and one label; size is a prop because Home sets the star larger than the shared ScreenHeader.
name: StarLink

description: Three small stars — the divider setting a revealed card's quote apart from its meaning, in place of a plain rule.
name: StarRule

description: A single card with a 3D flip: shows the back until flipped, then rotates to reveal the artwork. Rotates ONE face and swaps its image at the halfway point, because react-native-web flattens the 3D context and a stacked hidden face bleeds through.
name: TarotCard

description: EL CAROT set large in the display face, the app saying its own name. Home only; other screens carry the small version inside ScreenHeader.
name: Wordmark

description: Adds a day to the trail, newest-first and never twice for the same date — re-adding a day replaces it rather than duplicating the row.
name: addToHistory

description: Looks a card up by its arcana index, falling back to the first card when a stored index is out of range — corrupt or hand-edited storage should not crash the screen.
name: cardByIndex

description: The card belonging to a date. Deterministic via a cheap string hash rather than randomness, so the day's card survives a reinstall and can be asserted in a test.
name: cardForDate

description: Resolves a `?n=` deep-link parameter to a card, or null when the link names none. Junk, decimals, negatives and out-of-range indices all yield null so a bad link opens the spread instead of erroring.
name: cardFromParam

description: Resolves a card's artwork filename to its bundled image, falling back to the card back for an unknown name. React Native's bundler needs literal require() calls, which is why the map is written out rather than built from a path.
name: cardImage

description: Picks a card's meaning or quote in the requested language, falling back to the Spanish original when a translation is missing so an untranslated card still says something.
name: cardText

description: Caps a slot's distance-from-centre at one card in either direction, so cards further out in the spread stop accumulating tilt and dimming instead of ending up upside-down and invisible.
name: clamp

description: Renders a stored YYYY-MM-DD as the day a card belonged to, in the reader's language. Capitalizes only the first letter in Spanish, which lowercases weekdays and months — CSS `capitalize` would give "Lunes, 20 De Julio".
name: formatDay

description: Back navigation that survives a deep link. `router.back()` is a no-op when there is no history to pop — exactly what happens when a screen is opened directly by URL — so it falls back to an explicit destination and the back control keeps working either way.
name: goBackTo

untestabilityReason.justification: A two-line expo-router boundary: it reads router.canGoBack() and calls back() or replace(). There is no logic of its own to assert — only the router calls, which need a live navigation stack. Its effect is visible in every ScreenHeader scenario.

description: Loads today's state, rolling the day over when the stored draw is stale: a stale draw that was revealed graduates into history, while one never opened is dropped, on the reasoning that an unopened card was never really yours.
name: loadDaily

description: Past days only — today's card lives in the daily draw, not here. Returns an empty list rather than undefined before any day has rolled over.
name: loadHistory

description: Computes the slide-in menu panel width for a given screen width — a fixed share of the screen, capped at a maximum column width so it does not sprawl on wide screens.
name: menuPanelWidth

description: Flips today's card face-up and records it, so a reload keeps it open. Returns a new draw rather than mutating the one passed in.
name: revealToday

description: Returns the whole 22-card deck in random order for the pick-your-own-card spread. Fisher-Yates over a copy, so the shared source deck is never reordered. Shuffling is what makes choosing a face-down card a real draw rather than picking an index.
name: shuffledDeck

description: Computes the three animation samples for one slot in the card spread — rail one card left, at rest, one card right — giving its position, drop, tilt and opacity. Guarantees an arriving neighbour lands in the exact centre pose, which is what removes the visible snap when moving between cards.
name: slotSamples

description: Binds one spread slot's animation samples onto the rail's Animated.Value, producing the interpolated opacity and transform the carousel renders.
name: slotStyle

untestabilityReason.justification: A React Native Animated boundary — it only feeds pre-computed samples into Animated.Value.interpolate, which needs a live animation driver to observe. The arithmetic it binds lives in slotSamples (lib/cardPickerLayout.ts) and is fully unit-tested there.

description: Looks up a UI string in the current language. The copy lives beside the language it belongs to, so a missing key is a type error rather than an `undefined` on screen.
name: t

description: Formats a date as YYYY-MM-DD in LOCAL time, so the day turns over at the user's midnight rather than Greenwich's. Zero-padded so keys sort lexically.
name: toDateKey

description: The current day, honouring a seeded `todayOverride`. That override is the scenario seam — never written by the app — so a capture taken months later still renders the day it was authored for.
name: today

description: Loads today's card, its date, whether it has been revealed, and how many past cards exist. Reveal is optimistic — the card turns immediately and the write follows — and is a no-op on an already-open card or before the load lands.
name: useDailyCard

description: Reads the current language and its setters from context. Living in context is what lets the toggle on one screen update every screen at once.
name: useLang

untestabilityReason.justification: A one-line useContext read with no logic of its own. What it returns is provided by LangProvider, whose behaviour is shown by the langprovider-default scenario and asserted through the `t` and formatDay tests.

description: Reads the app menu open/close context — the accessor the header star and overlay use to share menu state.
name: useMenu

untestabilityReason.justification: One-line hook returning useContext(MenuContext); the open/close state it exposes lives in MenuProvider, which is scenario-covered.

description: Turns a chosen card face-up a beat after it arrives, so the flip is watched rather than already done. Resets to face-down on every change of card, including a direct swap, so a new card never inherits the previous one's revealed state.
name: useTurnUp
